name: First 5K
description: You crossed your first finish line. Everyone remembers their first.

name: First 10K
description: Double the distance, double the accomplishment. You're a real runner now.

name: First Half Marathon
description: 13.1 miles. The distance where strangers cry at the finish line and nobody thinks it's weird.

name: First Marathon
description: 26.2 miles. You are a marathoner. Nobody can ever take that away from you.

name: Sub-25 5K
description: Under 25 minutes. You're faster than most people who call themselves runners.

name: Sub-20 5K
description: Under 20 minutes. That's a 6:26 pace. You've entered a different category of runner.

name: Sub-50 10K
description: Under 50 minutes for 6.2 miles. Consistent and strong.

name: Sub-2 Half Marathon
description: Under 2 hours for 13.1 miles. A milestone every distance runner chases.

name: Sub-4 Marathon
description: Under 4 hours. You're in the top 30% of all marathon finishers. The training paid off.

name: Sub-3:30 Marathon
description: Under 3 hours 30 minutes. Boston qualifying territory. You're legit.

name: Sub-3 Marathon
description: Under 3 hours. Elite amateur territory. Fewer than 5% of marathoners ever do this.

name: One Week Streak
description: Seven consecutive days of training. The habit is forming.

name: 30-Day Streak
description: Thirty days of consistent training. This isn't a phase anymore. This is who you are.

name: Century Club
description: 100 lifetime miles. Your first century. Many more to come.

name: 500 Mile Club
description: 500 lifetime miles. That's roughly the distance from New York to Pittsburgh. On foot.

name: Thousand Miler
description: 1,000 lifetime miles. You've run the equivalent of crossing Texas. Your shoes certainly didn't make it.

name: Proper Footwear
description: You bought real running shoes. The cotton shirt can wait, but the feet come first.

name: Looking the Part
description: Your first apparel upgrade. You're starting to look like a runner, not just someone who jogs.

name: Sole Survivor
description: You wore a pair of shoes down to nothing. This is a rite of passage.

name: Did Not Finish
description: Not every run ends at the finish line. But you learned something. Rest up. The road will be there tomorrow.

name: Personal Record
description: You've never run this fast. Remember this feeling. You earned every second of it.

name: Race Veteran
description: Five races in the books. You know the pre-race jitters, the mid-race suffering, and the post-race high.

name: Serial Racer
description: Ten race finishes. Your medal collection is getting heavy.

name: 5K Completionist
description: You've run every 5K course available. The local running community knows you by name.

name: Built to Last
description: Endurance stat reached 30. Your aerobic base is solid. Time for longer distances.

name: Finding Your Speed
description: Speed stat reached 30. Those intervals are paying off.

name: Mind Over Miles
description: Mental Toughness reached 30. You've learned that the body quits long before it has to.

name: Top 10
description: A top 10 finish in any race. You're not just finishing anymore. You're competing.

name: On the Podium
description: Top 3 finish. Stand tall. You earned that spot.

name: First Place
description: You won a race. Broke the tape. Crossed the line first. This changes how you see yourself.

name: Good Samaritan
description: You stopped to help a fellow runner. The time cost was worth it. This is what the community is about.

name: Trail Runner
description: Finished a trail race. Dirt under your nails, mud on your shoes, grin on your face.

name: Tough Feet
description: Finished a race with an active blister. Your feet hate you but your heart is proud.

name: Barkley Fun Run
description: Completed 3 loops of the Barkley. Only a handful of humans have done this. You are not normal.

name: Big Eater
description: Used fuel every single segment of a 50K+ race. Your gut is an engine. Your nutritionist is proud.
